Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Megadev: A Development Kit for the Sega Mega Drive and Mega CD Hardware 49 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Baochip-1x: What It Is, Why I'm Doing It Now and How It Came About 109 upvotes, 16 comments
  3. Can I run AI locally? 1292 upvotes, 319 comments
  4. Python: The Optimization Ladder 50 upvotes, 8 comments
  5. Show HN: Channel Surfer – Watch YouTube like it’s cable TV 548 upvotes, 161 comments
  6. Michael Faraday: Scientist and Nonconformist (1996) 21 upvotes, 2 comments
  7. Mouser: An open source alternative to Logi-Plus mouse software 357 upvotes, 110 comments
  8. I found 39 Algolia admin keys exposed across open source documentation sites 139 upvotes, 40 comments
  9. You gotta think outside the hypercube 85 upvotes, 23 comments
  10. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 880 upvotes, 338 comments
  11. Hammerspoon 307 upvotes, 115 comments
  12. Atari 2600 BASIC Programming (2015) 39 upvotes, 9 comments
  13. The Isolation Trap: Erlang 89 upvotes, 30 comments
  14. Secure Secrets Management for Cursor Cloud Agents 21 upvotes, 4 comments
  15. Philosoph Jürgen Habermas Gestorben 9 upvotes, 1 comments
  16. Games with loot boxes to get minimum 16 age rating across Europe 250 upvotes, 155 comments
  17. AEP (API Design Standard and Tooling Ecosystem) 23 upvotes, 8 comments
  18. A Survival Guide to a PhD (2016) 136 upvotes, 79 comments
  19. Recursive Problems Benefit from Recursive Solutions 33 upvotes, 16 comments
  20. XML Is a Cheap DSL 105 upvotes, 70 comments
  21. Qatar helium shutdown puts chip supply chain on a two-week clock 643 upvotes, 553 comments
  22. Digg is gone again 257 upvotes, 249 comments
  23. RAM kits are now sold with one fake RAM stick alongside a real one 78 upvotes, 66 comments
  24. The Browser Becomes Your WordPress 15 upvotes, 9 comments
  25. Please Do Not A/B Test My Workflow 127 upvotes, 138 comments
  26. Coding after coders: The end of computer programming as we know it? 159 upvotes, 234 comments
  27. Show HN: AgentArmor – open-source 8-layer security framework for AI agents 6 upvotes, 3 comments
  28. I beg you to follow Crocker's Rules, even if you will be rude to me 97 upvotes, 148 comments
  29. Wired headphone sales are exploding 235 upvotes, 394 comments
  30. Mothers Defense (YC X26) Is Hiring in Austin 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 10:30pm, 14 March 2026. Source here.
